---
title: "Homework 3"
---

Welcome to my homework page! Below is my re-edited Homework 3 project for data analysis.

## Introduction
*(Type a brief sentence or two here explaining what this homework assignment was about.)*

## Data Analysis

Here is the code and analysis for my project:

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false

rm(list = ls())
library(tidyverse)
load("naic_asset.rta")
temp <- MyData |> select(COCODE, COMPANY_NAME, COMPANY_TYPE_DESC, CURRENT_YEAR, YEAR, LINE)
longdata <- temp |>
  filter(LINE%in%c("Bonds", "CashHandDeposit", "Stocks_Com", "Total"))


widedata <- longdata %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = LINE,
    values_from = CURRENT_YEAR
  ) %>%
  filter(Total > 0)

widedata <- widedata %>% 
  mutate(
    bondRatio = Bonds / Total, 
    stockRatio = Stocks_Com / Total, 
    YEAR = as.factor(YEAR),
    COMPANY_TYPE_DESC = as.factor(COMPANY_TYPE_DESC)
  )

longdata <- widedata %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Bonds, CashHandDeposit, Stocks_Com, Total, bondRatio, stockRatio),
    names_to = "name", 
    values_to = "value"
  )

# Part 2

plot1 <- longdata %>%
  filter(name == "Total") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YEAR, y = value)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + labs(title = "Total Asset Trends Over Time", y = "Total Assets")

print(plot1)

plot2 <- longdata %>%
  filter(name == "Total") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YEAR, y = value, color = COMPANY_TYPE_DESC)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Total Asset Trends by Company Type", y = "Total Assets")

print(plot2)

plot3 <- longdata %>%
  filter(name == "Total") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YEAR, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Total Assets by Year", y = "Total Assets")

print(plot3)

plot4 <- plot3 + 
  ylim(0, 108) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Total Assets (Zoomed 0-108)")

print(plot4)

plot5 <- longdata %>%
  filter(name == "bondRatio") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YEAR, y = value, fill = COMPANY_TYPE_DESC)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Bond Ratio Distribution by Company Type", y = "Bond Ratio")

plot6 <- longdata %>%
  filter(name %in% c("bondRatio", "stockRatio")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YEAR, y = value, fill = COMPANY_TYPE_DESC)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  facet_grid(name ~ COMPANY_TYPE_DESC) + # Facet by Ratio Name and Company Type
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Bond vs Stock Ratio Distributions", y = "Ratio Value")
print(plot6)

plot7_data <- longdata %>%
  filter(name %in% c("bondRatio", "stockRatio")) %>%
  # Pivot to wide to calculate means easily
  pivot_wider(names_from = name, values_from = value) %>%
  group_by(YEAR, COMPANY_TYPE_DESC) %>%
  summarize(
    bondRatio = mean(bondRatio, na.rm = TRUE),
    stockRatio = mean(stockRatio, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  # Pivot back to long for plotting
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(bondRatio, stockRatio),
    names_to = "name",
    values_to = "value"
  )
plot7 <- plot7_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YEAR, y = value, fill = name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~ COMPANY_TYPE_DESC) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Bond and Stock Ratios by Company Type", 
    x = "Year", 
    y = "Average Ratio"
  )

print(plot7)
```
---
